# freeswitch_operator (Resource)

Binds a Keycloak identity (subject) to a SIP extension. The webphone BFF resolves the logged-in user's extension via this mapping. RBAC roles live in Keycloak, not here.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Binds a Keycloak identity to a SIP extension. The webphone BFF looks up the
# logged-in user's `sub` here to resolve which extension they register as.
# RBAC roles (agent/supervisor/admin) live in Keycloak, not in this resource.

resource "freeswitch_user" "reception" {
  domain   = "example.com"
  number   = "1001"
  password = var.reception_sip_password
}

resource "freeswitch_operator" "reception" {
  subject      = "a1b2c3d4-0000-1111-2222-333344445555" # Keycloak user `sub`
  domain       = freeswitch_user.reception.domain
  number       = freeswitch_user.reception.number
  display_name = "Reception"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `domain` (String)
- `number` (String) SIP extension this identity registers as.
- `subject` (String) Keycloak `sub` (or username); unique.

### Optional

- `display_name` (String)
- `enabled` (Boolean)

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String)
- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `updated_at` (String)

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# freeswitch_operator is imported by its Keycloak subject.
terraform import freeswitch_operator.reception a1b2c3d4-0000-1111-2222-333344445555
```
